---
title: "Bioinformatics Basics"
format: html
---

Welcome to the official website of the **Bioinformatics Basics** course at the Faculty of Chemistry, Warsaw University of Technology!

::: {.callout-important}
## 🪰 2026 Theme: The Year of the Fruit Fly (*Drosophila melanogaster*)
This year, our main theme is the fruit fly! Why? [cite_start]Because this organism has recently become the protagonist of an absolute breakthrough at the intersection of biology and computer science: the first digital brain has just started walking [cite: 433-434].

[cite_start]A team at Eon Systems PBC, led by senior scientist Philip Shiu, has demonstrated the world's first embodied whole-brain emulation[cite: 439]. [cite_start]In 2024, Shiu and collaborators published in *Nature* a computational model of the entire adult fruit fly brain—consisting of 125,000 neurons and 50 million synaptic connections—built from the FlyWire connectome[cite: 442]. 

[cite_start]Now, using the NeuroMechFly v2 framework and MuJoCo physics simulation, scientists have integrated this digital brain with a virtual fly body[cite: 444]. [cite_start]The biology was copied neuron by neuron, and the digital fly simply "woke up" and started walking without any training data or machine learning algorithms [cite: 447-449]. This is a powerful testament to the future of *in silico* biological simulations!
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## New Curriculum – "From Sequence to System in the Era of AI"
The course has undergone a major update! Instead of manual database searching, you will learn how to automate your workflow using **R** and **Python** scripts (via Jupyter/Colab). We have also introduced cutting-edge topics such as NGS data analysis and protein structure prediction using Artificial Intelligence (AlphaFold).
:::

## 👨‍🏫 General Information
* **Instructor:** Arkadiusz Gładki, PhD, Eng.
* **E-mail:** arek at gladki.pl
* [cite_start]**Course Hours:** 30 (including laboratory classes and lectures) [cite: 384]

## 🧬 Course Description
[cite_start]Bioinformatics is a relatively young, dynamically developing, interdisciplinary field of science that aims to solve biological problems using modern information technology[cite: 376].

[cite_start]During the classes, we will present methods and strategies for effective searching of biological databases available online[cite: 377]. Throughout the course:
* [cite_start]You will become familiar with tools for sequence alignment and searching for potential homologs[cite: 378].
* [cite_start]You will understand concepts such as local and global alignment, Hidden Markov Models (HMM), substitution matrices, PSSM matrices, and statistical values (E-value, score, identity)[cite: 379].
* [cite_start]You will learn to identify functional and regulatory elements of the genome (including Open Reading Frames - ORF)[cite: 380].
* [cite_start]You will explore services gathering information on genomes, genes, and metabolic pathways (e.g., KEGG and COG databases)[cite: 381].
* [cite_start]We will cover the evolution of protein structure prediction—from classical homology modeling to the latest machine learning algorithms (AlphaFold)[cite: 382].

## 🎓 Grading & Requirements
To complete the course, the following conditions must be met:
1.  [cite_start]**Attendance:** Participation in laboratory classes is mandatory[cite: 391].
2.  [cite_start]**Final Exam:** The grade is based on a final test[cite: 388].
    * [cite_start]The exam is conducted on the Moodle platform (100 minutes)[cite: 389].
    * [cite_start]Format: Multiple-choice questions and 2-3 descriptive questions[cite: 390].

## 📋 Lab Schedule

Throughout the course, we will use data from [osteosarc.com](https://osteosarc.com/) — a publicly available multi-omic dataset from an osteosarcoma patient, including WGS/WES, RNA-seq, single-cell RNA-seq, and imaging data. This grounds every lab in a real clinical context.

| Lab | Topic | Key Tools | osteosarc.com Data |
|-----|-------|-----------|---------------------|
| 1 | Databases & APIs | NCBI, UniProt, rentrez (R), Biopython | Exploring TP53/RB1 via API |
| 2 | Sequence Alignment Algorithms | Biostrings, Needleman-Wunsch, Smith-Waterman | TP53 sequences across human, mouse, and dog |
| 3 | Profiles, BLAST & Evolution | BLAST, E-value, HMM | — |
| 4 | NGS Data & Variant Calling | FASTQ/BAM/VCF, VariantAnnotation (R) | Somatic variants from WES (TP53/RB1) |
| 5 | MSA, Phylogenetics & AlphaFold | T-Coffee, phylogenetic trees, AlphaFold | — |
| 6 | Gene Expression Analysis (RNA-Seq) | DESeq2, Volcano Plot | Bulk RNA-seq count matrix |
| 7 | Drug Design (Molecular Docking) | Molecular Docking, PDB | MDM2 protein as a therapeutic target |

## 📚 Additional Materials & Curiosities
To expand your knowledge beyond the classroom, feel free to explore the following resources:
* [cite_start]Free Online Book: [The Art of R Programming (Norman Matloff)](https://diytranscriptomics.com/Reading/files/The%20Art%20of%20R%20Programming.pdf)[cite: 407].
* [cite_start]Cloud Workstation (25h of free computing per month): [Posit Cloud](https://rstudio.cloud/)[cite: 415].
* [cite_start]Genetics Milestone: The 2022 complete human genome sequencing via the [T2T method](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.abp8653)[cite: 414].
* [cite_start]Recommended AI Course: [Machine Learning via Python Notebooks by Andrew Ng](https://www.coursera.org/specializations/machine-learning-introduction)[cite: 416].
